anchor.snippet: The first term is the product of the given sine and radius of the desired arc divided by the cosine of the arc
which in Europe was conventionally called Gregory's series after James Gregory
The text also contains Madhava's infinite series expansion of π which he obtained from the expansion of the arc-tangent function

annotations.label: Approximations of π to 11 and 13 decimals

anchor.snippet: Jyeṣṭhadeva gave values of π as 3.14159265359, correct to 11 decimals
obtain a rapidly converging series by transforming the original infinite series of π
The other method was to add a remainder term to the original series of π
